Learned counsel for the petitioner has approachecl this court for post arrest bail on hardship grounds only. The petitioner has ctttttenelerl that he was arrested on 14,12.16 and has been in custorly for {1 months i.e 3 years and 5 months and that no delav in the trial has been causetl either by himself or his counsel and as the completion oi the trial is r-ro vvhcrt' irr sight he is entitled to post arrest bail on hardship grounds. 5. On the other hand learned Special prosecutor NAB has opprrsetl the grant of bail to the petitioner on hardship grounc-ls as accorcling t() hinl the petitioner does not meet the legal requirements for the grant of bail on hardship grounds. In support of his contentions he has placed reliance on Tallat Ishaq v. National Accountability Bureau (PLD 2019 Su preme Court 112) 6. We have heard the parties, carefullv reviewer{ the recorr.i.rntl considered the relevant case law,including that cited at the bar. 7. The petitioner has been in custocly for 3 years anci 5 mouths. The dairy sheets reveal that no delay has been causecl bv thc pctitioner or counsel acting on his behalf. So far only 5 PW's out of a total of 22 havc been examined which leaves 1,7 PW's yet to be examinetl. f here arc also l5 accused in the reference and as such each PW will be subject to 15 separate cross examinations. This in our view will take at least one vear antl probably a 1ot longer. It is well settled that trail cannot be witlrheltl as a punishment and certainly in our view the petitioner cannot be. blarleel for the lack of progress in the trial. 8. With regard to the case of Tallat Ishaq (Supra) which N A B has citecl in its objection to granting bail. No doubt Tallat Ishaq,s case (Supra) has matle the grant of bail more stringent on harclship grounc.ls but it has not excluded it and it is allowed in exceptional circumstances at tht, discretion of the court. No doubt Tallat Ishaq,s case (Supra) has matle the grant of bail more stringent on harclship grounc.ls but it has not excluded it and it is allowed in exceptional circumstances at tht, discretion of the court.
